course_description.overview: Amateur companion race to the pro UCI Rund um Köln classic, on closed roads through the hilly Bergisches Land region, starting and finishing at Rheinauhafen in Cologne.

key_segments.name: Schloss Bensberg
key_segments.description: Iconic cobblestone climb to Bensberg Castle with crowds, punchy ascent.

key_segments.name: Bergisches Land Hills
key_segments.description: Series of short, steep climbs and rolling terrain throughout the route.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Constant up-and-down with numerous short, punchy climbs making it challenging for amateurs; diversified hilly profile.

logistics.getting_there: Start/finish at Rheinauhafen, Cologne; accessible by public transport, car, or bike; expo at Harry-Blum-Platz May 16-17.
logistics.accommodation: Abundant hotels in Cologne; book early for race weekend.
logistics.registration_details: Opens November 2025; online via rundumkoeln.de; multiple distances available.
logistics.bike_logistics: Standard road bike; transport to Cologne via train/plane/car.
logistics.local_considerations: Closed roads; family-friendly with kids' events; watch weather in May (mild, possible rain).

final_verdict.one_liner: Classic German amateur road race sharing pro roads with punchy hills and great atmosphere.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Ambitious road cyclists seeking a challenging gran fondo on closed roads with classic vibes.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for the prestige, scenery, and pro-level experience at amateur level.
